title: Echo
content:
"Who called?" I said, and the words
    Through the whispering glades,
 Hither, thither, baffled the birds - 
    "Who called? Who called?"

 The leafy boughs on high
    Hissed in the sun;
 The dark air carried my cry
    Faintingly on:

 Eyes in the green, in the shade,
    In the motionless brake,
 Voices that said what I said,
    For mockery's sake:

 "Who cares?" I bawled through my tears;
    The wind fell low:
 In the silence, "Who cares? who cares?"
    Wailed to and fro.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter De La Mare, titled "Echo", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
